title: Tardon Golor

{{Quote|Thieves like Tardon Golor are clever, and thus more dangerous than a thug who comes barging in, blasters blazing. Instead, Golor looks for a hole in a security system and then takes whatever he can before we have even discovered his presence. Not only do we lose the ships, but they often end in opposition to our interests.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Tardon Golor''' was a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] thief, who operated during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Golor even managed to steal [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-Wing]] and [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] from the New Republic.

==Biography==
Tardon Golor was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]]. A [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Thief/Legends|thief]], Golor once worked with a young [[Niles Ferrier]], and the pair learned their trade together. Golor did not particularly care what ships he stole; depending on the demand for a particular ship, he stole both military and civilian vessels. He would subsequently sell the ships on to [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] or those who supplied [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] or criminal organizations. Golor was wanted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] for the theft of several New Republic [[Starship/Legends|starships]], including several [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and a pair of [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|A/SF-01 B-wing starfighters]]. Intelligence reports indicated that Golor attempted to contact the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in order to sell his ships and components. The New Republic offered a reward of 18,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Golor's capture on the charges of theft of New Republic property and theft of civilian property.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Tardon Golor was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who was two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall with elaborately [[Tattoo/Legends|tattooed]] [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]]. He was shrewd bargainer, [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]], and [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Golor was skilled in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and [[blaster artillery]], and was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], cultures, and languages. Golor had an understanding of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] procedure, and knowledge of [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] practices. Golor knew how to contact the criminal underworld, determine the value of items, and use stealth techniques effectively.<ref name="WBC" />

Golor was a skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], capable of flying [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. He could operate the weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] systems on the vessels, and [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] the ships through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Golor could also drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles. His technical skills included [[Computer/Legends|computer]] programming and repair, [[Droid/Legends|droid]] programming and repair, transport and starfighter repair, starship weapon repair, and bypassing security systems.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Golor was armed with a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], and carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], [[pocket computer]] with [[Scomp link/Legends|scomp link]]. The computer was pre-programmed with three [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump coordinates and was also used to override starship security programs.<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tardon Golor was profiled in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], published in [[1993]] by [[West End Games]]. [[Mike Jackson]] supplied the image for Golor.
